title: K-200 System Architecture Document v1.0
text:
# K-200 System Architecture Document

Document number: MD-DOC-401-A. Revision: A. Type: specification. Author: Dr. Rhea Voss. Created: 2023-03-31. Last modified: 2023-04-10. Status: Released.

Critical review by: Dr. Rhea Voss (REV-631, 2023-03-28, Approved with comments — REV-631 contains essential rationale for architecture decisions that must be preserved).

## §2 Programme Overview

Product: Kestrel K-200 autonomous maritime survey drone. Manufacturer: Meridian Dynamics. MTOW: 8kg. Endurance: 60 minutes minimum. Configuration baseline: ECN-301.

## §3 Autonomy

### §3.2 Autonomy Level

The K-200 targets SAE Level 3 conditional automation. The selection of Level 3 rather than Level 4 (full autonomy) is driven by customer requirement RFT-K200-002, which explicitly prohibits fully autonomous navigation in mixed-traffic maritime zones without a human supervisory override. This is a contractual constraint, not a cost optimisation. Architecture decisions downstream of this constraint (continuous datalink requirement, GCS human-in-the-loop design) are binding. This was agreed at the WIKI-003 programme kick-off meeting. See REV-631 (Dr. Voss) comment C01 for the full rationale.

## §4 Redundancy and Fail-safes

### §4.1 GNC Redundancy

Single-string FCU architecture (P-1091, no redundancy). This was a deliberate trade against the 8kg MTOW constraint — a redundant FCU would add ~300g and exceed MTOW. Approved for BVLOS open-water operations only. Hardware fail-safe: if datalink lost >10 seconds, FCU executes return-to-launch. This constraint is documented in risk register R-GNC-004. Any proposal to extend K-200 operations to urban or littoral zones must revisit this trade. See REV-631 comment C02.

## §5 Comms Architecture

### §5.3 Datalink Range

Requirement: 15km. Implementation: 900MHz FHSS (P-1125, subsequently upgraded to P-1126 with AES-256 encryption per ECN-322). The 15km range requirement is the tightest constraint on comms architecture — if it changes, the antenna gain, frequency, and power budget must all be re-analysed. See REV-631 comment C03.

## §7 Mass Budget

MTOW: 8kg. Current design mass: 7.66kg. Margin: 340g (subsequently reduced to 290g after wing skin material change ECN-307). Margin tracked as programme risk R-SYS-001.
